hero.name: Alastair Lewis
hero.tagline: Frontend architecture, accessibility, and building things that scale.

about.label: Background
about.title: About Me
about.p1: Senior Software Engineer at Shopify. I build frontend architecture and design systems, the kind of work where a clean abstraction can save a thousand engineers an hour each. Polished UIs at serious scale. That's the stuff.
about.p2: Got my start at IBM, where I shipped across airlines, banking, nonprofits, and a moonshot security project through Extreme Blue. Lots of domains, one takeaway: great frontend is equal parts craft and systems thinking.
about.p3: Toronto-based. Golfer, skier, wannabe house DJ. I like to work remotely around the world, ideally within range of a first tee and a flat white.
about.stack: React, Angular, TypeScript, GraphQL, Node.js, Ruby on Rails, Python, Java, C/C++

projects_data.quantumflow_desc: A real-time collaborative data-pipeline editor with drag-and-drop node composition, live execution preview, and shareable pipeline URLs.
projects_data.meridian_desc: A design-system component library with zero runtime CSS-in-JS, full keyboard navigation, WCAG 2.2 AA compliance, and auto-generated Storybook docs.
projects_data.cascade_desc: A developer productivity CLI that analyses pull-request diff size, flags review complexity, and suggests optimal reviewer pairings using a graph-based model.

shopify_role1_bullets: Led frontend architecture for Shopify's admin design system, adopting CSS Modules and Polaris tokens across 40+ components.
Partnered with accessibility team to achieve WCAG 2.2 AA compliance across critical checkout flows, measured via axe-core CI gates.
Mentored three junior engineers through structured pairing, code reviews, and quarterly growth planning.
Drove adoption of a component ownership model that reduced cross-team regression incidents by ~30%.
shopify_role2_bullets: Built and maintained React components serving Shopify's merchant-facing analytics dashboard, handling 50M+ monthly active merchants.
Migrated legacy class components to hooks-based architecture, cutting bundle size by 18% and improving test coverage to 85%.
Collaborated with product and design on a redesigned order management interface that reduced merchant task completion time by 22%.
ibm_role1_bullets: Developed full-stack features for IBM OpenPages, an enterprise GRC platform, using React, Java, and DB2.
Redesigned the user-permissions module UI, reducing configuration time from 12 steps to 4.
Integrated REST APIs with third-party compliance data providers, automating previously manual regulatory reporting workflows.
ibm_role2_bullets: Built internal data-visualisation dashboards using D3.js and React for the IBM Watson Health division.
Participated in an agile team of 8, contributing to two-week sprint cycles and bi-weekly stakeholder demos.
Achieved IBM Cloud Professional Developer certification.
